key_insight: Conditioning each synaptic weight’s stochastic diffusion to stay on its consolidated side of a barrier turns intrinsic analog noise from a liability into a stabilizing drift for continual learning.
engineering_relevance: For neuromorphic hardware, the result suggests device noise can be exploited as a built-in consolidation mechanism, potentially improving retention without adding digital regularization overhead.
method: Model per-synapse weight dynamics as a Doob h-transform conditioned on never crossing a memory-critical barrier.
Add the induced drift term sigma^2 d/dw log h, which acts as a noise-amplified restoring force near the barrier.
Compare against anchored OU/EWC/MESU baselines and test on Split-MNIST and BrainScaleS-2 hardware.
evidence_signal: On single-head Split-MNIST, the method improved retention by 10.9 points at an interior noise optimum; on BrainScaleS-2 silicon it retained a prior task 15.6 points better than the matched control at similar average accuracy.
watch_item: Results on hardware are reported with a single seed, so broader robustness, scaling behavior, and chip-to-chip variability still need validation.
paper_title: Intrinsic-Noise Consolidation: A Doob-Barrier-Conditioned Diffusion Turns Analog Device Noise into a Continual-Learning Resource
